name: Western Union Money Order Only
description_short: Western Union is a global money transfer service offering international and domestic fund transfers, currency exchange, and bill payment services across 200+ countries.
description_long:
Western Union is one of the world's largest money transfer networks, operating for decades as a trusted provider of international remittance and domestic fund transfer services. The company enables consumers to send and receive money across more than 200 countries and territories, making it a primary option for families separated by borders and business transactions requiring cross-border payments.

Western Union's core service offerings include online money transfers with competitive exchange rates, international remittance with multiple currency options, and domestic US money transfers. The platform features dynamic pricing with fees ranging from $1.99 to $0.00 USD depending on transfer method and promotional offers. New users can register for better exchange rates and fee waivers on first international transfers. The service supports transfers in dozens of currencies including MXN, GTQ, PHP, INR, COP, and major global currencies across all inhabited continents.

What distinguishes Western Union is its extensive global reach—the website lists payment corridors to nearly every country worldwide, from Afghanistan to Zimbabwe. The company offers transparent fee structures and real-time exchange rate quotes before transfer completion. Their online platform allows users to compare send amounts against receiver payouts, enabling informed decisions about transfer costs. The registration incentive ($0 fee on first transfer) provides entry value for new international senders.

However, potential users should recognize that Western Union's fees and exchange rates, while competitive, may not always be the lowest available compared to newer fintech transfer services. The website content does not detail pickup locations, delivery timeframes, customer service responsiveness, or regulatory compliance details. The temporary unavailability of Orange Wallet noted on the site suggests occasional service disruptions. For price-sensitive senders or those requiring same-day domestic transfers, alternative providers warrant comparison.
pros: Global reach covering 200+ countries and territories with support for 150+ currencies
Transparent fee structure with rates displayed before transfer ($1.99 to $0.00 USD range)
Registration incentive offering $0 fee on first international online transfer
Real-time exchange rate quotes (e.g., 1.00 USD = 16.9486 to 17.6326 MXN)
Online platform allowing users to compare send amounts against exact receiver payouts
Multiple major currency support enabling transfers to most global destinations
cons: Exchange rates may not be competitive compared to specialized fintech money transfer services
Website shows service disruptions (Orange Wallet temporarily unavailable)
No information provided on delivery timeframes, pickup locations, or transfer speed options
Limited details on customer service availability, dispute resolution, or support channels
No clarity on minimum/maximum transfer amounts or account verification requirements
best_for: International remittance senders supporting family members abroad
Individuals requiring global money transfers across 200+ country corridors
Users seeking established, brand-trusted money transfer with transparent pricing
diagnosis: Western Union is best for international remittance senders and those requiring established global money transfer services across 200+ countries with transparent, competitive pricing. Primary caveat: newer fintech competitors may offer lower fees or better exchange rates for price-sensitive users, and the website lacks critical details on delivery times, service levels, and customer support.
